Gert Vanmechelen DELETE THIS PAGE FOR CUSTOMERS Table of Contents 1 Standard Service Description 6 1.1 Introduction 6 1.2 What is the added business value? 6 1.2.1 Enabling the new world of hybrid work 6 1.2.2 Efficient communication platforms 6 1.2.3 Internal and external collaboration 7 1.2.4 Centralized information management 7 1.2.5 Access to personal data, anytime, anywhere on any device 7 1.3 Which services are included? 8 1.3.1 Standard services 8 1.3.2 Optional services 8 1.3.3 Responsibility matrix 9 1.4 SKU overview 10 1.4.1 Service SKUs 10 1.4.2 Standard changes SKUs 11 1.4.3 Non-standard changes SKUs 12 1.5 Service Level Agreement 14 1.5.1 Service Support window 14 1.5.2 Specific service levels 15 1.6 Conditions 15 1.7 Out of scope 15 1.8 Cross-functional services 15

The “My Online Collaboration” Service ensures the operational management of
solutions in the Digital Workplace.

In today’s world, productivity isn’t about being in the office from 09.00h to 17.00h anymore – it’s about getting things done. The hybrid work context supports the workforce to be productive from anywhere, at any time and on any device. This requires the right set of tools to empower the end-user. Meaning, taking advantage of the benefits of the cloud, such as connectivity & availability, but also an always up-to-date platform, centralization of data, mobile applications, data protection, integration, …
In the current, rapidly evolving, SaaS-context, continuous platform management and configuration improvements are key in maintaining a healthy Workplace environment.
This Service ensures the proper operation and continuity of the communication & collaboration platform and it’s fundamentals.

One of the fundamental requirements for great teamwork is efficient communication. Productivity today is greatly improved by conversations – whether it’s a quick chat message, a call, an online meeting or an email. The mix of text, voice, video and content sharing in a centralized environment (preserving only “one version of the truth”) is strongly leveraging the experience for the end-user. It allows them to connect with colleagues, customers, partners, … more easily and efficiently.
This Service ensures that the communication-related features and functionalities are available via a reliable SaaS platform.

Collaborative applications are business-critical assets. There’s more than one way to organize collaboration with peers, a team or a workgroup.
This Service offers the management of all components necessary to ensure that collaboration, whether it’s ad-hoc or highly structured, can happen in a secure context, inside or outside the organization.

Where teamwork is happening, content is being created and edited in all kinds of formats: documents, lists, video, images, minutes, templates, … Whether the content is personal, restricted to a team, corporately available or shared externally, the key to success relies in the manageability of the platform and findability of the information. Therefore, functionalities like file sharing, versioning and version history, metadata, approval workflows, document templates, a search engine, security and permissions, offline use, … are an absolute must to keep the overview and structure.
This Service manages the components needed to offer these information management functionalities to the end-user.

With the new workplace, users will use a personal cloud space for their personal professional data. This enables users to share data more easily with colleagues, securely accessing their personal data from anywhere and on any device. The data itself is also available for offline use. This enables the user to access their personal data even in an environment without internet connectivity.
Cegeka will provide the following standard functionalities and corresponding activities in the “My Online Collaboration” Service:
This Responsibility Matrix provides the appropriate RACI tags for the Customer and Cegeka with respect to the activities for the Standard and Optional Services.

Activity CEGEKA customer standard services Reporting on activity & storage use Reporting and providing recommendations on guest & external access settings L2/L3 technical incident handling = Responsible: Party responsible for performing the activity and/or achieve the task. One party is Responsible, although other Parties can be delegated to assist in the actual work if required. An Accountable must sign off (approve) on work that Responsible provides. Responsible is by default proactive. = Accountable: Party ultimately accountable for the correct and thorough completion of the task and/or activity as performed by Responsible. For each task and/or activity maximum one Accountable must be specified. Accountable has the mandate to accept/dismiss on work provided by Responsible. Accountable is by default reactive. = Consulted: Party to be consulted (whose opinions are sought in two-way communication) when performing tasks and/or activities. Consulted may also provide requirements on scope, result expectations, etc. Consulted is by default reactive. = Informed: Party to be informed/kept up-to-date on tasks and/or activities (just one-way communication). Informed often only on completion of the task and/or activity. Informed no formal role in realization and therefore by default reactive.

| Monthly SKU | DESCRIPTION | Unit OF MEASUREMEnt | | My Online Collaboration – User * | Providing monthly operational reporting recommendations based on Operational Reports Technical Support for Inicidents | Per user |
Monthly SKU DESCRIPTION Unit OF MEASUREMEnt My Online Collaboration – User * Providing monthly Per user * mandatory SKU Measurement The pricing of the Service is user-based within a tenant. A tenant is an isolated, individual and virtualized section of the public cloud vendor platform, identified by a unique URL and ID, and a single user repository/licensing system. A user is identified by an enabled and assigned Microsoft 365/Office 365 license. Calculation (“Price/Unit” x “Number of users”)

This service makes use of a third party SAAS provider. For the SLA’s concerning uptime and/or availability of the 3rd party platform we refer to the following documentation  Service Level Agreements (SLA) for Online Services
In order to guarantee the Service quality, Cegeka refers to the cross-functional Services from the contract document set and their corresponding standard KPI's.
